page: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor architecture running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. For memory, the instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local NVMe storage and relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48574.86 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11551.0. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 952.0 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.17 GB/sec. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 1892.97 GB/sec, the PassMark memory score is weak. For graphics and specialized workloads, the instance achieves average results in Geekbench ray tracing and image processing. Large language model inference on a medium 7B model registers at 31.98 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 15.24 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5g.8xlarge leverages custom ARM64 silicon to deliver dedicated compute resources and GPU acceleration at a lower hardware-class profile compared to traditional x86 GPU instances. However, the lack of local storage and weak single-core performance represent clear architectural tradeoffs. This instance is suited for workloads such as Android game streaming, media encoding, machine learning inference on small-to-medium models, and GPU-accelerated ARM-native applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and a single NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and provides a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU and GPU-accelerated performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 11551.0 and average 7B LLM inference speeds, though single-core metrics and memory scores are relatively weak. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for ARM-native graphics workloads, media transcoding, and small-to-medium model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU, designed for cost-efficient ARM-native graphics and inference workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for graphics-intensive tasks.
tagline: An ARM64-based graphics instance featuring dedicated Neoverse cores and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for cost-efficient accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
Integrated NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in multi-core benchmarks and medium-model LLM inference
Suited for ARM-native graphics, media transcoding, and lightweight machine learning
